rulings.Case Title: QIAN & NEMECEK LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. PETER M. RADIN JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance By Laurel Spe, Llc With Subpoena Duces Tecum And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $10,365.41
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 19, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF QIAN & NEMECEK LLP'S Motion To Compel Compliance By Laurel Spe, Llc With Subpoena Duces Tecum And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $10,365.41 Pro Tem Judge J. Stephani Krmpotic, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel is continued 30-days, in light of the debtor's exam scheduled for March 19, 2019 in Department 514. Based on the papers submitted, objections to the broad categories of documents requested are not waived; however, it does not appear that Laurel SPE, LLC has made a sufficient effort to meet and confer and produce documents in categories that may relate to interests held by Defendant Radin. Laurel SPE, LLC is urged to produce relevant documents related to defendant Radin. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to JSKrmpotic@LOWBALL.COM with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: J. Stephani Krmpotic

rulings.Case Title: RONALD HANSEN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MUNI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Tax Defendant'S Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 19, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF RONALD HANSEN, DEANNA HANSEN's Motion To Tax Defendant'S Costs. Plaintiff Ronald Hansen's motion to tax defendant's costs is denied. The Court finds that the challenged items in the City's memorandum of costs were reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation. (Code Civ. Proc. § 1033.5; Ladas v. California State Automobile Ass'n (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 774 [upholding order allowing attorney service messenger and delivery charges]; see also Foothill-De Anza Community College Dist. v.. Emerich (2007) 158 Cal.App.4th 11, 30 [same day messenger fees].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES AUSTIN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 19, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT RENKA PROP LLC, ANNE KIHAGI's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively For Summary Adjudication. Defendants Anne Kihagi, Renka Prop, LLC, and Julia Mwangi's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, for summary adjudication is denied. Contrary to Defendants' contention, Plaintiffs James Austin and Raymond Schreiber's causes of action accrued, not when the Owner Move-In Notice was provided to Plaintiffs, but upon discovery by Plaintiffs that Ms. Mwangi had ceased occupying the unit. Plaintiffs properly pled delayed discovery by showing that they were ignorant of the facts that suggested the eviction was noticed in bad faith until June 2016, when they learned that Ms. Mwangi had left the unit prior to completing 36 months of occupancy as required by SF Rent Ordinance section 37.9(a)(8)(i) and started living outside California. (See Casualty Insurance Company v. Rees Investment Company (1971) 14 Cal.App.3d 716, 720 ["The rule is that plaintiff must plead and prove the facts showing: (a) Lack of knowledge. (b) Lack of means of obtaining knowledge (in the exercise of reasonable diligence the facts could not have been discovered at an earlier date). (c) How and when he did actually discover the fraud or mistake."].) THE COURT'S FULL TENTATIVE RULING IS SENT BY EMAIL TO THE PARTIES. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES **NICOLE TATE-NAGHI VS. DAVID NAGHI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Renewed) Motion To Transfer And For Sanctions.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 19, 2019, Line 15, (Renewed) Motion To Transfer And For Sanctions. Defendants' motion to change venue is granted. This action is transferred to the central district of Los Angeles County and plaintiff is to pay all required transfer costs. The proper county for commencement of a proceeding concerning a living trust is the county where the principal place of administration of the trust is located. (Prob. Code § 17005(a)(1).) Plaintiff has failed to provide a declaration that establishes the principal place of administration of the trust, defined as "the usual place where the day-to-day activity of the trust is carried on by the trustee or its representative who is primarily responsible for the administration of the trust." (Prob. Code § 17002(a).) The declaration of Mr. Salama shows only that when he meets in person to conduct trust business with plaintiff, those meetings occur in San Francisco. The declaration is silent regarding the frequency of these meetings or whether the day-to-day operations of the trust are always conducted in such in person meetings. The undisputed evidence shows that plaintiff trustee resides in and works in Los Angeles. The court reasonably infers based on plaintiff's residence and place of work that the trust is administered in Los Angeles. (See Prob. Code § 17002(b)(1) [if the principal place of administration cannot be determined, venue is at the trustee's residence or usual place of business].) Defendant's request for sanctions is denied because there was a reasonable basis for filing this action in San Francisco. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants are required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: RAJU VEGESNA VS. JOHN DAVID HAMNER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 19, 2019, Line 6, PLAINTIFF RAJU VEGESNA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues. Plaintiff Raju Vegesna's motion for summary judgment is granted. The undisputed evidence shows that defendant Hamner entered into the promissory notes and failed to make payment by the date of maturity. (Undisputed Facts 1-8.) Defendant Hamner's fraud defense fails. First, the defense is not pleaded in his answer. "[A] defect in the answer may entitle the moving party plaintiff to a summary judgment if the only matter in dispute is a defense that has not been intelligibly asserted in the answer." (FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 382.) Second, the defense fails. Plaintiff was the person that lent the money. The alleged investment agreement was a separate matter and the promissory notes do not refer to it. Third, defendant's evidence (Hamner declaration and attached e-mails) fail to show "fraud." The court will execute plaintiff's proposed order granting summary judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DERRICK SUTHERLAND VS. BAART PROGRAMS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 19, 2019, Line 13, DEFENDANT BAART PROGRAMS, INC., ADDICTION RESEARCH AND TREATMENT, INC., BAYMARK HEALTH SERVICES, TERESA FLEMING, NADINE ROBBINS-LAURENT's Motion To Compel Arbitration. Defendants BAART Programs, Inc., Addiction Research and Treatment, Inc., Baymark Health Services, Teresa Fleming, and Nadine Robbins-Laurent's motion to compel arbitration is granted and the action is stayed pending the conclusion of arbitration proceedings of all causes of action stated in plaintiff Derrick Sutherland's complaint. Defendants have met their burden to show the existence of an agreement to arbitrate between Mr. Sutherland and BAART Programs, Inc. Plaintiff's arguments that although he signed the arbitration agreement, he did not "consent" to it because he was required to sign it as a condition of employment and that he did not knowingly waive his right to pursue his claims in court are meritless. The agreement expressly states that the applicant agrees that if he is offered and accepts employment, any controversy, claim or dispute between him and BAART Programs "will be submitted to final and binding arbitration," and further states, in bold font and capital letters, "BY AGREEING TO THIS BINDING ARBITRATION PROVISION, IF I AM OFFERED AND I ACCEPT EMPLOYMENT WITH BAART PROGRAMS, BOTH I AND BAART PROGRAMS WAIVE ALL RIGHTS TO TRIAL BY JURY." Failure to read an arbitration agreement before signing does not relieve a party from the contractual obligations undertaken. (Rowland v. PaineWebber Inc. (1992) 4 Cal.App.4th 279, 286.) THE COURT'S FULL TENTATIVE RULING IS SENT BY EMAIL. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants are required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)
